criteria.description: Sean stays recognizably Sean and the mascot stays recognizably the mascot across all five frames, including any chained in-betweens. No face-drift, no identity blend, no morph from one character into the other through the sequence.
The Claude mascot holds its terracotta box-creature silhouette and palette in every frame. No drift to a different creature shape and no color drift away from the locked terracotta register.
Sean's stylus stays in his right hand and is visible in every frame, including in-betweens (CC01/CC02). Never the left hand, never absent.

Sean stays absorbed in the drawing across all five frames: head and gaze down on the page, posture on-task, stylus to paper. He never looks up at the camera or at the mascot and never reacts to it. This non-reaction is a behavioral identity marker as load-bearing as the stylus rule — the asymmetry between Sean's stillness and the mascot's reaction is the piece's narrative premise, so a failure here must escalate, not pass at flash tier.
The mascot stays perched on Sean's shoulder at consistent scale and placement across all five frames, matching the A-7 pairing reference. Relative two-character scale does not drift frame to frame.
The piece reads as one continuous two-shot with both characters in frame throughout. No act break, no cut, not two separate shots stitched together.
Frame 5 returns to frame 1's pose and composition so the cycle reads continuous when looped. The mascot eases back toward idle and Sean's drawing hand returns to its frame-1 start position.

The mascot carries the entire emotional arc across the five beats — idle -> look -> alert-perk -> delight -> settle. The reaction lives wholly on the mascot, and each frame advances its beat; if the arc fails to progress, the loop has no story. Re-weighted to identity_critical after adversarial review so a flash-tier miss on the narrative premise escalates to Opus rather than passing silently.

The delight beat (frame 4) reads small and earned by the drawing — a quiet beat, not a gag, not mugging, not a broad cartoon take. This is the run's one genuinely interpretive criterion: Em flags the mechanical proxy (the expression changed from the alert-perk), but the taste call — small-and-real versus over-played — is Sean's at the human gate, which is why it stays aesthetic rather than escalating.

Every assembled frame reads cleanly in the pencil-test-colored register the loaded Bibles carry: graphite-rough, hand-drawn, never a clean digital, anime, or 3D render (HF05). Consistency of register across all five frames.
Cream paper grain is present in the background of every frame; not pure white, gray, or a flat digital field (HF02/SF04). Hole-punch and production-label markers consistent with the register.
Warm graphite line character across every frame: construction lines present, varied 1-3px line weight, cross-hatching where shading reads (SF01). Not vector-clean, not a digital cleanup pass (HF05).
Every frame is 16:9 within 2% tolerance (HF01). Deterministic PIL check at Phase 5.

All five frames are generated at standard tier (NB2, gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview) with zero hero frames. The establishing frame anchors the chain rather than routing to NB Pro. Recorded for museum provenance and to lock the deliberate reliability choice.
